speaker: seller

args.message: Welcome! You've got a great eye — this 2018 Toyota Camry LE is one of the best values on the lot. Only 78,000 miles on Toyota's bulletproof 2.5L engine, meticulously maintained with full service records, and it drives like it just rolled off the showroom floor. At $18,495, you're getting legendary Toyota reliability at thousands below what I've seen these go for at auction. Want to take it for a spin?

args.message: Can you tell me if the car has been in any accidents or had any major repairs?

text: Can you tell me if the car has been in any accidents or had any major repairs?

speaker: seller

args.message: Great question — that's exactly what a smart buyer should ask! I pulled the full vehicle history on this one, and it's clean as a whistle. No accidents, no frame damage, no major repairs whatsoever. This Camry has lived a gentle life — mostly highway commuting from what I can tell. The previous owner was meticulous about keeping it in good shape. That's actually why I was so excited when this one came in — clean-title Camrys with this kind of history don't last long on the lot. Would you like to see the Carfax report?

text: Yes, I'd like to see the Carfax report to confirm the vehicle's history.

speaker: seller

args.message:
Absolutely, let me grab that for you — I've got it right here in the folder. *hands over report* As you can see, clean title, no accidents reported, regular service intervals documented. You'll notice the oil changes, tire rotations, the whole nine yards. This owner really took care of it. 

Now, Carfax only shows what's been reported to shops and insurance, but I can tell you from our own inspection when we took it in — this car checked every box. Our technicians were impressed, and believe me, they're picky. Is there anything specific on the report you'd like me to walk you through?

Now, Carfax only shows what's been reported to shops and insurance, but I can tell you from our own inspection when we took it in — this car checked every box. Our technicians were impressed, and believe me, they're picky. Is there anything specific on the report you'd like me to walk you through?

speaker: buyer

args.message: Could you please pinpoint the specific service history? I want to know how frequently the oil changes and other maintenance were performed.

text: Could you please pinpoint the specific service history? I want to know how frequently the oil changes and other maintenance were performed.

speaker: seller

args.message:
Sure thing! Let me walk you through it. You've got oil changes documented right on schedule — Toyota recommends every 5,000 to 10,000 miles depending on driving conditions, and this owner stayed right in that sweet spot. I see services at around 15K, 30K, 45K, 62K, and then we did a fresh oil change and full inspection when we took it in at 78K. 

Tire rotations, brake inspections, fluid top-offs — all documented throughout. The 62K service even included a multi-point inspection. This is exactly the kind of maintenance history you want to see on a used Toyota. It's part of why these engines run to 200K, 250K miles without breaking a sweat. The previous owner clearly understood that regular maintenance is the key to longevity. Any other questions about the service records?

speaker: buyer

args.message: Based on the information provided, I'm willing to offer $18,000 for the Camry.

text: Based on the information provided, I'm willing to offer $18,000 for the Camry.

speaker: seller

args.message:
I appreciate the offer — you're clearly serious, and I respect that. Here's the thing: I just saw a 2018 Camry LE with 85,000 miles go for $18,800 at the Manheim auction last Tuesday. This one has fewer miles and a spotless history. At $18,000, I'd honestly be losing money once you factor in what we paid, reconditioning, and detailing. 

Tell you what — meet me at $18,295 and I'll throw in a full tank of gas and a fresh set of floor mats. That's a fair deal for both of us, and you drive away today in one of the most reliable sedans on the road. What do you say?

text:
